description: Provider mode resolution and loop safety for PTY E2E scenarios, including mocked default, explicit real-provider opt-in, max iterations, timeouts, and fail-closed behavior.

userStories.title: Resolve provider mode with mocked default
userStories.description: As a Ralph agent, I want mocked or deterministic provider mode to be the default so that PTY E2E checks are repeatable and do not use live credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Provider mode defaults to mocked/deterministic when a scenario needs model output and --real-provider is not supplied
Scenario files cannot enable live provider calls by themselves without the CLI --real-provider flag
When mocked mode cannot be configured for a model-output scenario, the command fails with an actionable message
Provider mode is recorded in metadata.json
Focused provider mode tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Require explicit real-provider opt-in
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want real provider PTY runs to require explicit opt-in so that accidental provider calls do not happen.
acceptanceCriteria: Live provider mode starts only when --real-provider is present
Without --real-provider, a scenario requesting real provider mode fails before any live provider work starts
Failure output explains the --real-provider requirement
Tests verify real-provider scenarios fail without the flag
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce max iterations and stop conditions
userStories.description: As an operator, I want real-provider runs to be bounded so that model-backed agents cannot enter an endless loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Real-provider mode defaults maxIterations to 10 when no explicit value is supplied
Support explicit lower or higher maxIterations values from human CLI options
Real-provider mode requires an enforceable iteration counter or fails closed before starting
Iteration counting supports submitted user messages, observed assistant completions, or scenario steps marked iteration: true
Exceeding max iterations terminates the PTY process and records stop reason max_iterations
Tests cover default 10, explicit override, iteration limit violation, and fail-closed missing counter
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce real-provider timeout safety
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want real-provider scenarios to stop on timeouts and stop patterns so that live runs remain safe and inspectable.
acceptanceCriteria: Real-provider mode requires or defaults wall-clock timeout and idle timeout
Real-provider mode stops on expected output, explicit stop pattern, process exit, max iterations, wall-clock timeout, idle timeout, or external cancellation
metadata.json records max iterations, observed iterations, timeout values, and stop reason
Tests cover timeout stop reasons and stop-pattern completion
Typecheck passes
